content.text: Implement frontend logic to display informational and error messages related to social login and account access reset.
When logging in via email + password after social login reset:
but email is linked via social login → show error:
“This login method is inactive. Please use another one.”
and email is linked via social login → allow login.
Backend will provide the necessary flags / codes; frontend displays notifications accordingly.
During the first login / Google social linking, a flag is returned indicating that user data has been reset.
“Your account access method has been changed — personal data has been cleared for security reasons. Please re-enter it if necessary.”
Add all error and info messages to
When logging in via email + password:
If no password exists but social login is linked → display error
“This login method is inactive. Please use another one.”
If password exists and social login is linked → login is allowed.
During first social login / Google linking, display informational message:
“Your account access method has been changed — personal data has been cleared for security reasons. Please re-enter it if necessary.”
All messages are stored in Strapi and properly localized for all available languages.
Notifications are correctly displayed based on backend flags / codes.

issuetype.description: A task that needs to be done.
issuetype.name: Task

content.text: The goal of this task is to introduce a bonus tagging mechanism and enable bonus statistics aggregation by tags using the existing statistics service.
Review the existing client statistics service to understand how bonus data is currently tracked and stored in history.
Review the current bonus system to check whether any tag mechanism already exists.
If no tag system is present, it must be added.
(Optional) Add Backoffice endpoints to manage available bonus tags.
Implement functionality to assign a tag to a bonus during bonus creation or editing.
Implement the ability to retrieve statistics aggregated by client bonus tags.
Review the current reward calculation logic in the statistics service to understand how bonus reward sums are calculated based on the existing implementation.
Bonus statistics will not be recalculated retroactively (no historical backfill).
Bonus tags will be hardcoded on the Backend side for the first iteration to reduce Backoffice scope.
Tags must be implemented via a lookup entity to allow future extension or modification.
Tags must be stored in a separate table, not in the bonuses table.
All tag-related logic should be implemented directly in the statistics service.
A dedicated endpoint must be created in the statistics service to allow assigning a tag to a bonus.
An endpoint created for Backoffice must not only accept and pass bonus tag data, but also fully align with the expected Backoffice workflow.
When saving bonus changes in Backoffice (specifically when a tag is added or updated):
the endpoint must return the updated bonus data back to Backoffice as it currently does;
additionally, the same change must be persisted into the new bonus tag table within the statistics service.
This ensures data consistency between Backoffice bonus configuration and the statistics service storage.

issuetype.description: A task that needs to be done.
issuetype.name: Task

content.text: UI changes to inform players about real-balance-only gameplay through toast notifications, balance indicators, tooltips, balance popovers, and mobile UI updates.

This feature improves onboarding and retention by allowing players to access and play all games even when a bonus is active. Previously, some games were locked on the UI during an active bonus, which negatively impacted UX.

With this feature enabled and configured, players can play any game while the bonus is active. Bets placed in such games will use only the real balance, will not affect the bonus balance, and will not be counted towards wager contribution.

content.text: 1. Implement frontend logic to send the current user auth token when initiating social account linking and unlinking.
Add google to the UI in Client Profile.
When the user clicks “Link Google” in their profile, frontend must:
Retrieve the current auth token (session token used by the app).
Send this token to the backend endpoint that initiates social linking.
Backend will embed this token into  the OAuth request to the external provider.
Ensure correct handling for Google linking flows.
Frontend sends current auth token when initiating linking.
Backend receives token correctly and includes it into OAuth .
Linking flow works end-to-end for Google.
Error handling implemented (missing token, invalid token, etc.).
